      Smart $aver®: Home Improvement Rebate Program

      Home Improvement Rebates

      Get energy-efficient products installed and earn our highest rebates yet with Smart $aver.

      Find a Contractor

      How Smart $aver Rebates Work

      Whether you’re looking to replace aging equipment, improve air quality and comfort, or find ways to lower your energy bills, you’ve come to the right place. No matter your project, our Smart $aver program makes the home improvement process easy.

      • Choose Your Rebate Project, Find a Contractor

        Use Find it Duke to quickly connect with a participating contractor.

      • Make Your Improvements

        The contractor completes the work and submits your rebate application.

      • Get Your Rebates

        You choose your reward! Receive a physical card or a digital Mastercard® for your virtual wallet.

      Select Your Rebate

      • Heating and Cooling Systems‌

        Upgrade your HVAC to high performance, energy-saving equipment.

      • Heat Pump (Hybrid) Water Heater‌

        See how this advanced technology can significantly reduce your water heating costs.

      • Attic Insulation/Air Sealing & Ductwork‌

        Attic insulation, air sealing and ductwork improvements help lock in temperatures and improve air quality.

      • Variable-Speed Pool Pump‌

        Earn rebates on ENERGY STAR® certified variable-speed pool pumps.

      • Energy-Efficient Windows‌

        Boost home comfort and energy savings by replacing inefficient windows.

      For projects completed
      after 8/1/2025

      Eligible Equipment - (must be AHRI certified with ECM motors)

      Smart Thermostat
      Qualifying smart thermostat must be purchased along with eligible HVAC equipment through a participating Smart $aver contractor, installed, and programmed through customer's home Wi-Fi network at time of HVAC installation. $125

      **Existing heating system being converted must be a Heat Pump system with electric resistance strip heat serving a minimum of 1,000 sq. ft. of conditioned living space to be eligible for Heat Pump conversion rebates.

      Comfort today. Savings tomorrow.

      Why boosting your home's energy efficiency pays off.
      Increasing your home’s energy efficiency is a smart investment. It helps save energy, lowers your bills and creates a more comfortable, healthier space to enjoy every day.

      Smart $aver rebates make upgrading more affordable by offsetting upfront costs, helping you get more value from your investment. The long-term benefits are even greater:

      • Control energy costs year-round, season after season.
      • Lower your carbon footprint.
      • Reduce total energy consumption—less load on the grid benefits your community and impacts the planet.

      Upgrading your home’s essential systems won’t just help save on energy bills—it helps your home work smarter, not harder. Plus, these improvements protect your home’s value and will keep it resilient for years to come.

      Learn about other programs to help you save energy.
      Sign up for a free in-house energy assessment, designed to help you learn how your home uses energy and how you can save on your monthly bills. It's the first step on your journey to energy savings!

      Sign up online or call 844.346.4366 to schedule your FREE Home Energy House Call today.

      Have a question or concern?
      We’re here to help. Complete the Smart $aver feedback form and one of our specialists will be in touch.

      Save Energy and Money with Energy Saver North Carolina!

      Did you know there are extra rebates and assistance programs available for eligible homeowners and renters in North Carolina? Save money on your energy bill with energy efficient products and get financial assistance on home upgrades done by licensed contractors.

      See if you're eligible with a simple application!

      Frequently Asked Questions

      • Participating contractors are responsible for submitting rebate applications within 60 days after the work has been completed. Once approved, you will receive your rebate in the form of a physical prepaid Mastercard or a digital Mastercard for your mobile wallet. This process typically takes 4-6 weeks.

      • All contractors who are part of our trusted network of service providers are industry-licensed professionals. Contractors who participate in our Insulate and Seal programs are certified by either the Building Performance Institute (BPI) or the North American Technician Excellence (NATE) organization. Only our Smart $aver participating contractors are approved to perform services that will qualify for a rebate from Duke Energy.

      • As HVAC equipment ages, certain components that are critical to its operation will begin to fail. When this happens, the system will need to be replaced. Those critical components include the compressor, heat exchanger, and/or refrigerant charge. If the existing equipment has a failed compressor, cracked heat exchanger, or excessive refrigerant leak, the Replace on Failure rebate value will be applicable.

        If the existing equipment is being replaced prior to any of the above-mentioned critical components fail, the Early Replacement rebate value will be applicable. All Early Replacement rebate applications must include a customer-signed, Early Replacement Form attesting that the existing equipment does not have a critical component failure at time of replacement.

      • Conserving energy is the right thing to do for our customers and ourselves. The demand for electricity is growing, and building new power plants is expensive and takes years to complete. The cleanest, most efficient power plant is the one we never have to build.

      • The program is available to owners of individually metered residences including multi-family, single family detached, duplexes, townhomes, condominiums, and mobile homes, who are served on a Duke Energy residential electric service schedule.

      Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information (CA)

      Thank you for contacting Duke Energy. Because this right is available only to California residents, we will not be able to complete your request. For additional information about Duke Energy’s privacy practices, please review the Duke Energy Digital Privacy Policy.
      Duke Energy Privacy Policy

      Manage Consent Preferences

      Functional Cookies

      Always Active

      These cookies enable the website to provide enhanced functionality and personalisation. They may be set by us or by third party providers whose services we have added to our pages. If you do not allow these cookies then some or all of these services may not function properly.

      Performance Cookies

      Always Active

      These cookies allow us to count visits and traffic sources so we can measure and improve the performance of our site. They help us to know which pages are the most and least popular and see how visitors move around the site. All information these cookies collect is aggregated and therefore anonymous. If you do not allow these cookies we will not know when you have visited our site, and will not be able to monitor its performance.

      Strictly Necessary Cookies

      Always Active

      These cookies are necessary for the website to function and cannot be switched off in our systems. They are usually only set in response to actions made by you which amount to a request for services, such as setting your privacy preferences, logging in or filling in forms. You can set your browser to block or alert you about these cookies, but some parts of the site will not then work. These cookies do not store any personally identifiable information.

      Targeting Cookies

      Always Active

      These cookies may be set through our site by our advertising partners. They may be used by those companies to build a profile of your interests and show you relevant adverts on other sites. They do not store directly personal information, but are based on uniquely identifying your browser and internet device. If you do not allow these cookies, you will experience less targeted advertising.
